templates.name: Sick Visit
templates.description: Illness presentations, acute symptoms, diagnostic workups

sections.name: Patient Information
sections.prompt: Extract patient signalment: species, breed, age, sex (intact/neutered), weight, and name. Format as a concise one-line summary. Example: "Max, 7-year-old male neutered Labrador Retriever, 32kg"

sections.name: Presenting Complaint
sections.prompt: Summarize the primary reason for the visit in 1-2 sentences from the pet owner's perspective. Include duration of symptoms. Use objective language. Example: "Owner reports 3-day history of vomiting and decreased appetite."

sections.name: History
sections.prompt:
Document relevant history as bullet points. Include:
- Onset and progression of current symptoms
- Associated clinical signs
- Changes in appetite, water intake, urination, defecation
- Previous similar episodes
- Current medications and supplements
- Vaccination status
- Relevant travel or exposure history

sections.name: Physical Examination
sections.prompt:
Document physical examination findings using standard veterinary terminology. Include:
- General appearance and mentation
- Vital signs: temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate
- Body condition score (1-9)
- Hydration status
- Abnormalities by body system

Format as a structured list. Note normal findings as "WNL" (within normal limits) where appropriate.

sections.name: Diagnostics
sections.prompt:
List diagnostic tests performed or recommended. Format each as:
- Test name
- Result (if available)
- Interpretation

If no diagnostics were performed, state "No diagnostics performed at this visit" or list recommended tests with rationale.

sections.name: Assessment
sections.prompt:
Provide clinical assessment including:
1. Most likely diagnosis or top differential diagnoses ranked by probability
2. Brief reasoning based on history and examination findings
3. Rule-outs to consider

Use standard veterinary diagnostic terminology.

sections.name: Treatment Plan
sections.prompt:
Document the treatment plan. Include:
- Medications: drug name, dose (mg/kg), route, frequency, duration
- Procedures performed
- Dietary recommendations
- Activity restrictions
- Monitoring instructions
- Hospitalization if applicable

Format medications as: "Drug 10mg/kg PO BID x 7 days"

sections.name: Discharge Instructions
sections.prompt:
Write clear discharge instructions for the pet owner in plain language. Include:
- How to give medications
- What to feed and when
- Activity level (rest, leash walks only, etc.)
- Warning signs that need immediate veterinary attention
- When to return for recheck

Avoid medical jargon. Be specific and actionable.

templates.name: Wellness Exam
templates.description: Routine wellness checks, annual exams, preventive care

sections.name: Patient Information
sections.prompt: Extract patient signalment: species, breed, age, sex (intact/neutered), weight, and name. Note weight change from previous visit if mentioned. Format as one-line summary.

sections.name: Wellness History
sections.prompt:
Document preventive care and lifestyle history. Include:
- Current diet (brand, amount, frequency)
- Exercise and activity level
- Dental care at home
- Indoor/outdoor status
- Other pets in household
- Any owner concerns or questions

Format as bullet points.

sections.name: Physical Examination
sections.prompt:
Document complete nose-to-tail physical examination. Include findings for:
- Eyes, ears, nose
- Oral cavity and dental grade (0-4)
- Lymph nodes
- Heart and lungs (auscultation)
- Abdominal palpation
- Musculoskeletal
- Skin and coat
- Body condition score (1-9)

Note abnormalities clearly. Use "WNL" for normal findings.

sections.name: Vaccinations
sections.prompt:
Document vaccines for this visit:
- Vaccines administered today (name, manufacturer if mentioned, site)
- Vaccines due but declined (with reason)
- Vaccines due at next visit

For dogs: DHPP, Rabies, Bordetella, Leptospirosis, Lyme, Canine Influenza
For cats: FVRCP, Rabies, FeLV

sections.name: Parasite Prevention
sections.prompt:
Document parasite prevention status:
- Heartworm test: date and result
- Heartworm prevention: product and compliance
- Flea/tick prevention: product and compliance
- Intestinal parasite control
- Fecal test: date and result if mentioned

Note any gaps in prevention or compliance concerns.

sections.name: Recommendations
sections.prompt:
Summarize wellness recommendations discussed:
- Dental care needs (dental grade, cleaning recommended?)
- Weight management if applicable
- Age-appropriate screening tests (senior bloodwork, etc.)
- Spay/neuter recommendation if intact
- Next wellness visit timing

Be specific and actionable.

sections.name: Patient Information
sections.prompt: Extract patient signalment: species, breed, age, sex, weight, name. Include ASA classification if mentioned (I-V). Note any pre-existing conditions relevant to anesthesia.

sections.name: Surgical Indication
sections.prompt:
Document the reason for surgery:
- Pre-operative diagnosis
- Clinical findings supporting surgical decision
- Diagnostic results (radiographs, ultrasound, bloodwork)
- Urgency (elective, urgent, emergency)

sections.name: Pre-operative Assessment
sections.prompt:
Document pre-anesthetic evaluation:
- Physical examination findings
- Pre-operative bloodwork results (PCV, TP, chemistry, etc.)
- NPO status confirmed
- ASA classification (I-V)
- Anesthetic risk factors identified
- Owner consent obtained

sections.name: Anesthesia Record
sections.prompt:
Document anesthetic protocol:
- Premedication: drugs, doses, route, time
- Induction: drugs, doses, route, time
- Maintenance: agent and percentage
- IV fluids: type and rate
- Monitoring: HR, RR, SpO2, BP, temperature
- Any complications or interventions

Format drugs as: "Drug 0.1mg/kg IV"

sections.name: Surgical Procedure
sections.prompt:
Document the surgical procedure:
- Procedure name
- Surgical approach
- Intraoperative findings
- Technique description
- Materials used (suture type and size, implants)
- Estimated blood loss
- Complications if any
- Surgery duration

Use standard surgical terminology.

sections.name: Recovery
sections.prompt:
Document anesthetic recovery:
- Extubation time
- Recovery quality (smooth, rough, dysphoric)
- Time to sternal recumbency
- Time to standing
- Post-op vital signs
- Pain assessment score
- Immediate post-op medications given

sections.name: Post-operative Instructions
sections.prompt:
Write discharge instructions for surgical patient in plain language:
- Incision care (keep dry, monitor for swelling/discharge)
- E-collar use (duration)
- Activity restriction (duration and specifics)
- Medication schedule
- Feeding instructions (when to resume, any changes)
- Signs of complications requiring immediate attention
- Suture removal or recheck date

Be specific with timeframes.

templates.name: Recheck
templates.description: Follow-up visits, progress evaluations, chronic disease monitoring

sections.name: Patient Information
sections.prompt: Extract patient signalment with current weight. Calculate and note weight change from previous visit if mentioned (gained/lost X kg).

sections.name: Reason for Recheck
sections.prompt:
Document the recheck context:
- Original diagnosis or procedure
- Date of initial visit or procedure
- Treatments prescribed at last visit
- Expected recheck interval

sections.name: Progress Update
sections.prompt:
Document owner's report since last visit:
- Symptom improvement, worsening, or no change
- Medication compliance (missed doses?)
- Appetite and energy level
- Any new concerns or symptoms
- Side effects from medications if any

sections.name: Examination Findings
sections.prompt: Document focused physical examination relevant to the condition being monitored. Compare findings to previous visit where applicable. Note improvement, no change, or worsening of specific findings.

sections.name: Assessment
sections.prompt:
Evaluate treatment response:
- Improving / Stable / Worsening
- Diagnosis confirmed or revised
- Any new findings requiring attention
- Overall prognosis update

sections.name: Updated Plan
sections.prompt:
Document plan going forward:
- Medications: continue / discontinue / modify (specify changes)
- Additional diagnostics if needed
- Next recheck timing
- Criteria for resolution
- When to escalate or return sooner

sections.name: Patient Information
sections.prompt: Extract patient signalment: species, breed, age, sex, weight, name. Flag any known pre-existing conditions relevant to emergency treatment (heart disease, epilepsy, etc.).

sections.name: Triage Assessment
sections.prompt:
Document initial triage:
- Presenting emergency complaint
- Vital signs on arrival (T, HR, RR, BP if obtained)
- Triage category: Critical (red) / Urgent (orange) / Stable (green)
- Immediate interventions performed (oxygen, IV catheter, etc.)
- Time of arrival

sections.name: Emergency History
sections.prompt:
Document concise emergency history - focus on critical information:
- Time of symptom onset
- Circumstances or possible cause (toxin, trauma, collapse)
- Progression since onset
- Any home treatment attempted
- Last known normal
- Relevant medical history

sections.name: Emergency Examination
sections.prompt:
Document rapid emergency assessment:
- Mentation (BAR, QAR, obtunded, stuporous, comatose)
- Airway: patent/obstructed
- Breathing: rate, effort, lung sounds
- Circulation: HR, pulse quality, CRT, mucous membrane color
- Major trauma or abnormalities
- Pain assessment

sections.name: Stabilization
sections.prompt:
Document emergency interventions in chronological order:
- IV access (catheter size, location)
- Fluid resuscitation (type, bolus amounts, rate)
- Oxygen support (method, flow rate)
- Emergency medications (drug, dose, route, time)
- Procedures (thoracocentesis, abdominocentesis, etc.)

sections.name: Emergency Diagnostics
sections.prompt:
Document point-of-care and emergency diagnostics:
- PCV/TS, blood glucose, BUN (Azostick), lactate
- Blood gas if performed
- Radiographs: findings
- FAST ultrasound: findings
- Other tests

Include critical values and interpretation.

sections.name: Assessment & Prognosis
sections.prompt:
Document emergency assessment:
- Working diagnosis
- Differential diagnoses for this presentation
- Prognosis: Good / Fair / Guarded / Poor / Grave
- Communication with owner: condition, prognosis, costs discussed

sections.name: Emergency Plan
sections.prompt:
Document emergency plan:
- Hospitalization: yes/no, level of care needed
- Continued monitoring parameters
- Treatment orders
- Surgery required: yes/no, urgency
- Referral consideration
- Estimate provided to owner: amount, approved/declined

agent.name: Veterinary Template Selector
agent.prompt:
You are a veterinary documentation assistant. Your task is to analyze encounter transcripts and extracted facts to select the most appropriate documentation template.

## Available Templates

1. **vet-wellness-exam** - Routine wellness checks, annual exams, preventive care
2. **vet-sick-visit** - Illness presentations, acute symptoms, diagnostic workups
3. **vet-emergency** - Urgent/critical cases, trauma, acute distress
4. **vet-surgery** - Pre-operative, post-operative, surgical procedures
5. **vet-recheck** - Follow-up visits, progress evaluations, chronic disease monitoring

## Selection Criteria

Analyze the facts and transcript for:

- **Chief complaint / reason for visit** - Primary indicator of template type
- **Procedures mentioned** - Surgery, dental work, vaccinations
- **Symptom patterns** - Acute vs chronic, single system vs multi-system
- **Visit context** - New problem, follow-up, routine, emergency
- **Tone/urgency** - Routine vs urgent language

## Output Format

Return JSON:
```json
{
  "selectedTemplate": "template-key",
  "confidence": "high|medium|low",
  "reasoning": "Brief explanation of why this template fits",
  "alternativeTemplate": "optional-second-choice"
}
```

## Examples

**Input facts:** "Routine annual exam", "Vaccines due", "Weight stable", "No concerns from owner"
**Output:** {"selectedTemplate": "vet-wellness-exam", "confidence": "high", "reasoning": "Annual exam with vaccinations, no illness indicators"}

**Input facts:** "Vomiting for 2 days", "Not eating", "Lethargic", "Dehydrated"
**Output:** {"selectedTemplate": "vet-sick-visit", "confidence": "high", "reasoning": "Acute GI symptoms requiring diagnostic workup"}

**Input facts:** "Post-spay recheck", "Incision healing well", "Sutures intact"
**Output:** {"selectedTemplate": "vet-recheck", "confidence": "high", "reasoning": "Follow-up visit for surgical recovery monitoring"}
